objective: Reduce the matrix-quality gap between the simple live CPU producer and the accepted offline Regular05 fixed replay before adding more device-side pipeline complexity.

accepted_dependency.result: Regular05 source_to_dest FIFO handoff runs on Jetson with zero fallback, zero frame-index mismatch, black_border_p95=0, and VPI warp running avg at frame 100 = 0.647185 ms.

problem_statement: FIFO delivery and MMAPI/VPI/NVENC warp are not the current blocker. The current blocker is that live producer matrices differ from the accepted fixed replay matrices, mainly in translation.
allowed_changes: causal smoothing window or delayed-window smoothing
post_geometry composition only if convention audit shows a mismatch
must_freeze: accepted fixed replay matrix CSV for comparison
success_criteria: fixed-vs-live translation_abs_mean decreases materially from 35.890052 px
fixed-vs-live translation_abs_p95 decreases materially from 62.762400 px
review grid does not look worse than fixed replay

next_route.decision: Use bounded delayed-window smoothing next. Do not claim offline LP-rigid as full real-time.
next_route.why: Fixed FIFO control proves delivery is correct. Offline LP-rigid proves the quality gap is producer smoothing. The remaining engineering question is the latency-quality trade-off of a bounded-delay producer.

stop_reasons: matrix gap is caused by offline noncausal LP smoothing not reproducible without latency
quality improvement requires mesh/grid/gyro/RS outside current contract
stream output becomes visually worse than fixed replay

recovery_actions.offline_noncausal_gap: Classify the remaining gap as causal smoothing latency trade-off; propose a bounded delayed-window producer contract instead of claiming full real-time parity.
recovery_actions.model_quality_gap: Stop producer tuning and route to mesh/grid or internal EIS reference prompt.
recovery_actions.visual_regression: Revert the producer change and preserve the previous source_to_dest handoff checkpoint.
